response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering credentials with 20+ years of experience and proven backend systems expertise, they lack critical requirements for this executive CTO role. Most critically, there is no demonstrated production experience building and operating multi-agent AI systems—only a vague mention of "agentic workflows" at a current role (13 months) without concrete architectural details. Fintech infrastructure experience is entirely absent, and there is no evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people or operating in regulated/high-stakes financial environments. The candidate is a capable full-stack engineer but not a specialized fintech-AI executive.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience across multiple domains and technologies since 2006
Demonstrated backend and distributed-systems expertise with real-time systems (Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and caching)
Current involvement in AI infrastructure and agentic frameworks at re:cinq, showing recent interest and engagement in the required domain
concerns: No demonstrated production multi-agent AI system architecture or evidence of feedback loops, agent learning, and observability infrastructure
Completely absent fintech infrastructure experience—no background in credit systems, payment flows, algorithmic working-capital management, or regulated financial environments
No evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people or building internal technical leadership layers

key_factors.factor: Fintech domain expertise and regulatory experience
General engineering seniority and learning ability
